channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: EU’s “Loaded” Public Questionnaire Pushes Anti-Nicotine Agenda
item.description: The EU’s tobacco consultation is facing criticism for framing safer nicotine products through the same lens as cigarettes. By grouping vapes, nicotine pouches and combustible tobacco together, the questionnaire risks ignoring major differences in relative risk and real-world use. Consumer voices matter because regulation directly affects people trying to move away from smoking. A credible policy process should ask better questions before reaching restrictive answers. (Published on June 16, 2026byfiltermag.org)

item.title: Palau: How to Buy a Small Nation State
item.description: Palau’s push to place nicotine under international drug-control rules raises serious questions about influence, governance and proportionality. Treating all nicotine products like illicit drugs risks ignoring the major difference between combustible cigarettes and lower-risk alternatives such as vapes and pouches. For smokers, prohibition does not remove demand — it removes legal, regulated options. Global nicotine policy should be built on evidence and relative risk, not on campaigns that turn harm reduction into a drug war. (Published on June 16, 2026bythefirebreak.org)

item.title: OPINION: The Kubin report, how Brussels works, and the right thing to do.
item.description: The Kubin report has become another test of whether Brussels is willing to listen to consumers, evidence and real-world outcomes. Critics argue that EU nicotine policy is too often shaped by institutional process, political pressure and precautionary instincts rather than a clear assessment of relative risk. For smokers, the right thing to do is not to restrict safer alternatives until cigarettes remain the easiest option. Better regulation should protect young people while preserving access to products that can move adults away from combustion. (Published on June 16, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)
